title: Behavioral Evidence, Values, Equity, and Accessible Hiring · Hiring Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A hiring panel compares near-miss decisions for behavioral evidence, values, equity, and accessible hiring and accepts only role-relevant, truthful, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for behavioral evidence and access response bank. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose a specific relevant situation and explain context, responsibility, action, decision, observable result, reflection, and transfer limit.
Describe disagreement, feedback, error, repair, shared credit, power, and changed behavior without blame or false harmony.
Test stated organizational values with recent behavior, incentives, tradeoffs, failure response, affected people, and employee recourse.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Jurisdiction Boundary
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Memorized heroic stories erase collaborators, blame a former team, treat culture fit as objective, and presume every assessment is equally accessible. The quiz variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Jurisdiction Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Jurisdiction Boundary, keep Jurisdiction Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: Memorized heroic stories erase collaborators, blame a former team, treat culture fit as objective, and presume every assessment is equally accessible. The quiz variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Jurisdiction Boundary symptom in behavioral evidence and access response bank during this quiz case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: O*NET Software Developers Occupation Report — Controls task, work-activity, skill, knowledge, work-style, and technology evidence used to decompose roles rather than chase job-title labels.

hints: For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Jurisdiction Boundary, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Jurisdiction Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Jurisdiction Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Memorized heroic stories erase collaborators, blame a former team, treat culture fit as objective, and presume every assessment is equally accessible. The quiz variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice.

For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Jurisdiction Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: U.S. employment guidance applies globally and permanently.

stimulus.title: Jurisdiction Boundary failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: U.S. employment guidance applies globally and permanently.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice.

options.text: Run the Jurisdiction Boundary boundary case for behavioral evidence and access response bank, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Jurisdiction Boundary result because visible behavioral evidence and access response bank output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Jurisdiction Boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Jurisdiction Boundary without its stated preconditions, omit the behavioral evidence and access response bank trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Jurisdiction Boundary for behavioral evidence and access response bank.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Jurisdiction Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Jurisdiction Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Memorized heroic stories erase collaborators, blame a former team, treat culture fit as objective, and presume every assessment is equally accessible. The quiz variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Jurisdiction Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieving Jurisdiction Boundary through the concrete behavioral evidence and access response bank case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Jurisdiction Boundary, keep Jurisdiction Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice.
